task.objective: Converge PR #9 onto the current main baseline after PR #10 integration, resolve the validation-evidence merge conflict without dropping either task's evidence, and revalidate autonomous-execution governance against the post-assurance repository state.

requirements: Treat this as autonomous post-main convergence for PR #9. Continue through base integration, conflict diagnosis, conflict resolution, evidence reconciliation, validation, remediation, retries, commit, and push until BUILDER_READY_FOR_REVIEW or a true stop condition is reached.

Fetch origin and confirm the current main baseline contains merged PR #10 commit ecb10ed4723fce3d250f8a02468a4afadd638786 in its ancestry. If main has materially advanced beyond this baseline, inspect the additional commits before proceeding and stop only for a genuine authority or semantic conflict.

Integrate current origin/main into governance/autonomous-execution-defaults-1 using a history-preserving strategy chosen by the Builder. Do not force-push rewritten history unless necessary; prefer a normal merge when it preserves reviewability.

Resolve the docs/engineering/validation-evidence.md conflict by retaining the complete KERNEL-ASSURE-001 and KERNEL-ASSURE-CONV-001 evidence already on main and the complete OPS-AUTO-001 evidence from PR #9. Do not drop, rewrite as if newly executed, or conflate historical command evidence from either task.

Keep OPS-AUTO-001 autonomous-execution guidance materially unchanged unless post-main integration reveals a real authority conflict. Preserve Authority-Change Preflight, self-grant prohibition, independent review, product runtime authority separation, and Agent action != approved change != trusted release in substance.

Do not modify kernel, contracts, tests, scripts, workflows, dependencies, canonical terminology, Verdict semantics, Evidence authority classes, Trust semantics, protocols, architecture, product semantics, or runtime behavior.
After integration, inspect the diff from origin/main to HEAD and prove task-local changes are limited to OPS-AUTO-001 / OPS-AUTO-CONV-001 writable governance and evidence paths.

Re-run all OPS-AUTO-001 verification commands against the post-PR-#10 baseline. Record actual current kernel test counts from pnpm verify; do not retain stale 72-test wording as current convergence verification evidence.

Add a post-main convergence section to docs/engineering/validation-evidence.md that records base integration, conflict path, resolution policy, current verification results, exact origin/main comparison scope, and whether the merge/conflict resolution changed the intended OPS-AUTO governance text.

Update the Builder review with a post-main convergence finding covering the validation-evidence conflict and its disposition. Do not erase the original Builder findings or pretend the conflict did not occur.

Perform a deterministic pnpm verify no-mutation pre/post comparison using tracked diff hash and sorted status state after convergence changes are otherwise complete, and record exact values and sequencing limitations.
Do not claim independent acceptance, PASS, PASS_FOR_INTEGRATION, trusted release, product readiness, Phase 1 completion, or a Proofrail product Verdict.

requiredInvariants: Current origin/main is integrated before review readiness is claimed.
KERNEL-ASSURE evidence from main and OPS-AUTO evidence from PR #9 are both retained without historical evidence fabrication.
No product or runtime semantics change.
No packages or production code change is introduced by the OPS-AUTO convergence delta from current origin/main.
Human routing is not required for ordinary reversible choices within valid authority.
Autonomous remediation does not grant release or acceptance authority.
pnpm verify is non-mutating under the recorded deterministic pre/post comparison.
PR #9 becomes mergeable against the current main baseline before independent integration review.

requiredArtifacts: retained KERNEL-ASSURE and OPS-AUTO validation evidence
stopConditions: Authority-Change Preflight would need to be weakened
self-grant prevention would need to be weakened
independent review would need to be removed
the main integration conflict requires edits outside writable scope
repeated autonomous convergence reaches a deadlock
